author: y4rr
content:
Landed upstream in \`/home/doll/ferray/ferray-fft\` (0.3.1 → 0.3.2 workspace bump).

## What landed

### \`ferray-fft/src/hermitian.rs\`
Four new public functions:
- \`hfftn(a, s, axes, norm)\` — N-D inverse of \`ihfftn\`: complex Hermitian → real
- \`ihfftn(a, s, axes, norm)\` — N-D real → complex Hermitian
- \`hfft2(a, s, axes, norm)\` — 2-D variant; defaults to last two axes
- \`ihfft2(a, s, axes, norm)\` — 2-D variant; defaults to last two axes

Implementation: each delegates to existing \`rfftn\`/\`irfftn\` with input/output conjugation and a swapped norm (Forward ↔ Backward, Ortho preserved). Math is identical to the existing 1-D \`hfft\`/\`ihfft\` but generalized over arbitrary axis sets.

Refactored existing 1-D paths to use a new \`swap_norm\` helper for clarity (functional behavior unchanged).

### Re-exports
\`ferray-fft/src/lib.rs\` now re-exports \`hfft, hfft2, hfftn, ihfft, ihfft2, ihfftn\`.

### Workspace
Bumped \`workspace.package.version\` from 0.3.1 → 0.3.2 (ready for the user to \`cargo publish\`).

## Tests
8 hermitian tests pass (5 new):
- \`hfftn_ihfftn_roundtrip_2d\` — round-trip a 4x4 real signal end-to-end
- \`hfft2_ihfft2_roundtrip\` — same via the 2-D variants (default last-two axes)
- \`ihfftn_collapses_to_ihfft_for_1d\` — 1-D agreement with the existing \`ihfft\`
- \`hfftn_collapses_to_hfft_for_1d\` — 1-D agreement with the existing \`hfft\`
- \`hfft2_rejects_1d_input\` — error path for low-rank input

## Follow-up (post-publish)
Once \`ferray-fft 0.3.2\` is on crates.io, ferrotorch can:
1. Bump \`Cargo.toml\` line 64 (\`ferray-fft = \"0.3\"\` → \"0.3.2\") if a patch is needed.
2. Add public \`fft::hfftn\` / \`ihfftn\` in \`ferrotorch-core/src/fft.rs\` mirroring the existing \`hfft\`/\`ihfft\` wrappers (delegate to \`ferray_fft::hfftn\` / \`ihfftn\`).
3. Add matching differentiable wrappers \`hfftn_differentiable\` / \`ihfftn_differentiable\` in \`grad_fns/fft.rs\` (would naturally extend the work just landed in #580 from 1-D to N-D).

I'll do step 2-3 in a follow-up once you've published.
